title: Income Tax Department Clarifies Transition to Income Tax Act, 2025 for AY 2026-27 with New FAQs
content:
# Income Tax Department Clarifies Transition to Income Tax Act, 2025 for AY 2026-27 with New FAQs

Ek important update hai! The Income Tax Department has released a comprehensive set of Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) to provide clarity on the transition from the Income-tax Act, 1961, to the new Income Tax Act, 2025. These FAQs are crucial for taxpayers as we navigate the Assessment Year (AY) 2026-27.

Here are the key takeaways:

*   **Applicability for AY 2026-27:** For income earned during Financial Year 2025-26 (which corresponds to AY 2026-27), all provisions of the *Income-tax Act, 1961*, will continue to apply. This means you will use the old ITR forms and follow the procedures laid down in the 1961 Act for this assessment year.
*   **New Act from FY 2026-27:** The Income Tax Act, 2025, along with its new "Tax Year" concept, will become effective for income earned from Financial Year 2026-27 onwards. Returns for this 'Tax Year 2026-27' will be due in 2027.
*   **Dual Operation of e-filing Portal:** The Income-tax Department's e-filing portal will concurrently support compliance under both the old (1961) and the new (2025) Acts during this transition phase.
*   **No New Taxes:** The transition aims for simplification and clarity in the tax system, not the introduction of new taxes or an increased tax burden.
*   **Advance Tax for FY 2026-27:** Advance tax payments for income earned in Financial Year 2026-27 (which is 'Tax Year 2026-27' under the new Act) will be made in accordance with the provisions of the Income Tax Act, 2025, starting from June 2026.
*   **Loss Carry Forward:** Losses determined for AY 2026-27 under the 1961 Act can be carried forward and set off in Tax Year 2026-27 and subsequent tax years under the new Act, subject to prescribed conditions.
*   **Tax Audit Reports:** For FY 2025-26 (AY 2026-27), the tax audit report must be filed using the existing forms (Form 3CA, Form 3CB, and Form 3CD) prescribed under the Income-tax Act, 1961.

In essence, while the new Act is in force from April 1, 2026, the income of FY 2025-26 will still be governed by the erstwhile 1961 Act for all compliance purposes for AY 2026-27.

## Read the Official Document
* [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.incometax.gov.in/iec/foportal/help/faqs/faqs-interplay-and-transition)

*Published on: 27 June 2026*
